script: David Allen calls these open loops.

props.definition: Unfinished tasks your brain keeps tracking until it registers closure.
props.analogy: Like browser tabs left open in your mind, quietly draining battery.

script: I scan what's unfinished, write one line about where I'll pick it up

tips.text: Write one line on where you'll pick up tomorrow
Note one thing you actually finished

script: do your most dreaded task first — but only one frog
The done list corrects your perception of yourself.

props.before: To-do list: a permanent state of deficit, never hits zero
props.after: Done list: an honest picture of what you actually moved forward
props.label: Shift how you measure a day

script: The night before, write exactly three priorities for tomorrow.

props.title: Three Priorities — Written the Night Before

tips.text: Not five — five means you don't have to choose
Not ten — that's a task list, not priorities
Three. If the day falls apart, you know which one matters

script: What gets measured gets noticed. And noticing is most of the work.

props.quote: What gets measured gets noticed. And noticing, it turns out, is most of the work.
